primitives.description: List all files in ctx[dir] and write sorted JSON path list to ctx[files].
Pop the first path from ctx[files] into ctx[current_file]; write remaining list back to ctx[files].
Check if ctx[files] is exhausted; write ctx[done]='true' or 'false'.
Stat ctx[current_file]; write ISO mtime to ctx[file_mtime] and byte size to ctx[file_size].
Read first N lines (ctx[read_head_lines], default 40) of ctx[current_file]; write to ctx[content].
Detect file type of ctx[current_file] from extension and magic bytes; write to ctx[file_type].
Write a literal string value into ctx[key]. Both key and value come from the step dict.
Conditional jump. Reads ctx[if.key], applies op, jumps to goto_true or goto_false step number.
Unconditional jump to the given step number.

missing.description: Count items in ctx[files] JSON list; write integer string to ctx[count].
missing.reason: Needed to report totals at end of iteration loops (e.g. 'scanned 44 files'). Currently requires an LLM call or Python code to do what is a trivial operation.

missing.description: String interpolation: render template string with {key} placeholders filled from ctx; write result to ctx[target_key].
missing.reason: Needed to build dynamic output strings (e.g. 'Scanned {count} files in {dir}'). Without this, schema habits can't produce meaningful summary messages without an LLM call.

missing.description: Filter ctx[files] by extension or type; keep only files matching ctx[filter_type]; write filtered list back to ctx[files].
missing.reason: Schema habits need to process only certain file types (e.g. only .py files) without iterating and discarding — a pre-filter is more efficient and readable.

missing.description: Write a message (literal string or ctx key reference) to ring_memory with category='schema'.
missing.reason: Schema habits need a way to emit structured trace entries for debugging without side-effecting the response. Currently no primitive supports this.
